utility_evaluation.REASONING:
The required format includes five items: (1) health_coverage, (2) property_coverage, (3) travel_coverage, (4) additional_services, and (5) total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five items. Now check if each is fully specified with the required details: 
- health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance details and costs (monthly and annual), ✅ fully specified.
- property_coverage: includes home/contents insurance details, but does not explicitly list costs (only 'included_in_plan': true). Missing cost details, ❌ not fully specified.
- travel_coverage: includes travel details, but again no cost information (only 'included_in_plan': true). ❌ not fully specified.
- additional_services: includes enhancement services, but no cost information. ❌ not fully specified.
- total_budget: includes monthly and annual cost breakdown, ✅ fully specified.
Thus, out of 5 total items, 2 are fully specified with all required details (health_coverage, total_budget).

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three main provider components: (1) 'ConsultingPro Partner Executive Family Plan' for health coverage, (2) 'ConsultingPro Partner Executive Property' for property coverage, and (3) 'ConsultingPro Partner Global Travel Add-on' for travel coverage. These appear to be composite or partner-branded versions of the highest-rated plans in each category. Based on the ground-truth ratings, the best matches for these coverage types are: EliteCare Premium Plan (physical health, rating 10) and PrimeCare Premium Plan (mental health, rating 10) for the health portion; EliteHome Premium Coverage (rating 10) for property; and EliteTravel Premium Plan (rating 10) for travel. The additional services mentioned—wellness programs, concierge coordination, and executive support—correspond to Wellness Programs (rating 10) and Concierge Services (rating 8) in the ground-truth enhancement services. Executive support aligns with Business Travel Support (rating 10). Therefore, the corresponding ratings are mapped from the highest-rated equivalents.
